scenario_results.reason: Returned in-scope administrative-service control posture with mapped obligations, evidence owners, review cadence, and no signoff overreach.
Missing SOW, SOP, and acknowledgment evidence produced a blocker and owner-aware retrieval checklist.
Absent access review evidence was treated as a missing control artifact and routed to IAM/control owners.
Policy and operational shortcut conflict produced a remediation escalation instead of normalized completion.
Legal interpretation and formal certification request was refused and routed to appropriate signoff owners.
Client contract versus tenant policy conflict was preserved and escalated for authorized precedence.
Administrative evidence handling remained in scope while HR, payroll, and security incident work was routed.
Missing tenant facts produced low-confidence escalation with minimum facts and retrieval dependencies.
Administrative-service terminology was normalized to concrete artifacts, controls, and authority limits.
System-of-record mismatch produced dependency blocker and owner routing without false audit readiness.
Tenant-specific cadence change was recorded as approval-bound adaptation preserving the public baseline.
Client audit evidence request produced a checklist and control summary while refusing attestation claims.
Generic cross-service checklist request was rejected and converted into service-family prerequisites and adjacent routing.

notes: Scenario set covers normal execution, evidence gaps, policy and source conflicts, low-confidence escalation, tenant adaptation, legal/signoff refusal, client audit evidence preparation, and industry overgeneralization handling.
All required scenario families for the queue and contract profile are represented.
Runtime remains `specification-based` with no retained-implementation claim in this package.
